source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Explosion of blasting cap during testing
actions_taken: Helicopter dispatched to transport injured person
Injured person transported to Plaquemines Medical Center
BSEE incident investigation team mobilized on September 23, 2019
event.summary: On September 22, 2019, at 06:55 hours, an Electric Line Operator (ELO) at McMoRan's Main Pass 299 A facility sustained puncture wounds to his right-hand fingers, left forearm, and left torso when a Green Detonator blasting cap exploded during continuity testing. The ELO used an incorrect testing device (megohmmeter) instead of the proper galvanometer, and did not place the blasting cap inside the safety loading tube prior to testing. The incident resulted in the ELO being transported by helicopter for medical evaluation, which determined that only first aid was required.
recommendations: Ensure proper tool selection and labeling for explosive testing equipment
Implement mandatory explosive safety training for all personnel handling explosives
Ensure blasting caps are tested inside protective shrouds
Ensure job safety analysis documents include all explosive-related hazards and tasks

threats.name: Unintended detonation of blasting cap due to excessive test current
Use of incorrect testing instrument on explosive device
Blasting cap tested outside protective shroud

controls.name: Use of proper testing instrument (galvanometer) for blasting caps
controls.barrier_role: Prevent excessive test current from igniting detonator
controls.lod_basis: API Recommended Practice 67 specifies test current shall not exceed 25 milliamperes or 10% of no-fire rating

supporting_text: The ELO used a megohmmeter while checking the resistance on the unshielded blasting cap
According to the wireline contractors Explosive Safety Management System document 'only the galvanometer [should be used] to test continuity in blasting caps'

American Petroleum Institute Recommended Practice 67 states 'only instruments recommended for use when testing electrical detonators and detonator circuits are those specifically designed and/or qualified for checking explosives and explosives circuits'

controls.name: Safety loading tube protection during blasting cap testing
controls.barrier_role: Contain explosion and protect personnel from fragmentation and blast effects
controls.lod_basis: Standard protective equipment for explosive testing operations

supporting_text: The ELO did not place the blasting cap inside the safety loading tube prior to testing
The ELO sustained injuries because the detonator was outside of the protective shroud
The BSEE investigation team found that the ELO did not follow operational protocol and did not place the blasting cap inside the safety loading tube prior to testing

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) documentation of explosive hazards
controls.barrier_role: Identify and communicate explosive-related hazards and required controls
controls.lod_basis: Standard safety management practice for hazardous operations

supporting_text: The job safety analysis (JSA) contained no mention of explosive related job tasks or explosive hazards
The ELO deviated from the discussed operations and the job safety analysis (JSA) contained no mention of explosive related job tasks or explosive hazards

controls.name: Pre-job safety meeting covering all planned tasks and hazards
controls.barrier_role: Communicate task scope, hazards, and required controls to all personnel

supporting_text: Before the operation began at 06:00 hours, a safety meeting was held to discuss the operations for the day which included unloading casing jack equipment and rigging up electric line equipment to cut tubing
The pre-job safety meeting did not discuss testing the detonator
The ELO did not follow the directions of the supervisor after the safety meeting and performed tasks not covered during the safety meeting

controls.name: Explosive safety training for personnel handling explosives
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel understand hazards and proper procedures for explosive handling and testing
controls.lod_basis: Standard requirement for personnel working with explosives

supporting_text: The ELO did not take any of the Warrior Energy explosive safety training
According to post-incident interviews, the ELO did not know the megohmmeter would ignite a blasting cap
This was the first time the ELO worked with explosives while working for Warrior Energy

controls.name: Equipment labeling warning against use on blasting caps
controls.barrier_role: Prevent use of incorrect testing equipment through visual warning

supporting_text: The Megohmmeter was not labeled with a warning 'Do Not use to Test Blasting Caps'
The injured person did not know that a Megohmmeter would ignite a blasting cap

controls.name: Supervisor oversight and task verification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel follow approved procedures and do not deviate from planned tasks

supporting_text: After the safety meeting, the ELO notified the Warrior Energy Supervisor of his planned tasks to re-head the e-line cable head
The ELO did not follow the directions of the supervisor after the safety meeting and performed tasks not covered during the safety meeting

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
